text: 0.1g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a tuber, the cleanest win is a more stable carb profile.
20.1g of carbs. starch is doing most of the heavy lifting here. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.
1.6g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.
vitamin a at 160% daily value, vitamin c at 3% daily value stand out most. that is major vitamin support.
minerals are basically not part of the case here.

on the plus side: carotenoid content gives it extra value because that adds a useful plant-pigment angle beyond the basic carb role most tubers play. fairly filling for the calories because a practical strength for an energy food. easy to use as a wholefood staple because works cleanly in meals without needing much rescue from toppings or processing.

the drawbacks next: naturally sweeter than some tubers because still fine, but it is not the lowest-sugar energy food in the category. healthiness depends on preparation because heavy added fats or sugary toppings can change the practical outcome. protein contribution is light because like most tubers, it is not doing much in the protein lane.
S tier. Best when you want practical carbs and the rest of the profile is still reasonably clean.
